{"session_id":"9db3b0ed-c4d0-49f2-b669-b0f3e7bba980","transcript_path":"C:\\Users\\liam.bond\\.claude\\projects\\C--Users-liam-bond\\9db3b0ed-c4d0-49f2-b669-b0f3e7bba980.jsonl","cwd":"C:\\Users\\liam.bond\\Documents\\Productivity Tool\\dashboard","permission_mode":"plan","hook_event_name":"Stop","stop_hook_active":false,"last_assistant_message":"Research agent is running in the background doing the full sweep (GitHub, HN, Reddit, web). Will surface when done.\n\nWhile it runs, here's my honest assessment of **what you've actually lost** from all the recent changes combined:\n\n---\n\n## Functionality loss audit\n\n### Significant losses\n\n**`claude-mem` disabled** *(externally changed, not by us)*\nThis is the biggest one. The persistent memory system that saves facts about you, your projects, and preferences across sessions is now off. Primer.md still works as manual memory but Claude won't automatically accumulate and recall context between sessions. Worth re-enabling if you use it actively.\n\n**`document-skills` disabled**\nBlocks: `/docx`, `/pptx`, `/xlsx`, `/pdf`, `/slack-gif-creator`. If you generate reports, presentations, or spreadsheets through Claude, this needs re-enabling. Low overhead if you use it infrequently — just re-enable when needed.\n\n**Morning scan quality (Sonnet→Haiku)**\nHaiku handles the factual steps fine (search, extract, write) but the synthesis steps — cross-referencing new tools against your config manifest, spotting integration synergies, writing nuanced install recommendations — will be noticeably flatter. The scan will still work, just less insightful.\n\n---\n\n### Minor/negligible losses\n\n| What | Impact |\n|---|---|\n| Language rules (cpp, golang, kotlin, python etc.) | Only matters if you write in those languages — your stack is TS/Next.js/PS/bash |\n| `agent-orchestration` disabled | `agent-teams` still covers it |\n| `context-management` disabled | `claude-mem` (when on) + primer.md cover it |\n| `developer-growth-analysis` | You weren't using it |\n| `connect-apps` | You weren't using it |\n| Nightly loop 15→8 iterations | Marginal — quality of auto-generated rules slightly lower |\n| observe.sh replaced with bash logger | Less granular session data for nightly loop, but summaries are cleaner |\n\n---\n\n### Nothing lost\n\n- All your actual workflow tools (GSD, RALPH, PM skills, pr-review-toolkit, debugging, etc.) still enabled\n- TypeScript/Next.js/Angular work fully intact\n- Jira/Atlassian MCP unchanged\n- Git workflow, hooks, commit enforcement all unchanged\n- Dashboard, morning scan, nightly loop all still running (just cheaper)\n\n---\n\n**My recommendation before the research lands:** re-enable `claude-mem` — it costs very little in tokens but the cross-session memory is genuinely useful for your exploratory AI tooling work. Everything else is a reasonable trade.\n\nResearch agent will be back shortly with the full findings."}
